_notes.purpose: Iteration-4 commodity leaf for Vietnam → US textile-sector exports. Demonstrates 'any corridor + any commodity' via the existing _base + corridor + commodity 3-layer merge with no Python changes. dispatch=draps_v1 keeps the knot routed through the V1 DRAPS path so byte-equality for india-us-textiles stays green; the component values below are advisory in this iteration and become executed when a future profile sets dispatch=v2_direct.
_notes.iter4_demo_choice: Tariff inputs are held IDENTICAL to india-us-textiles (0.50 current / 0.60 peak) by design. The architectural point of Iteration 4 is that the corridor swap mechanism works; the ONLY corridor-level value that should visibly differ between vietnam-us-textiles and india-us-textiles in this iteration is the sovereign spread. Real Vietnam-specific tariff sourcing (e.g. the S&P-quoted 0.20 current / 0.46 peak figures from theinvestor.vn, Sept 7 2025) lands in Iteration 9 alongside live tariff binding, where the values are actually executed rather than advisory.
_notes.v1_runtime_discrepancy_tariff: Same as india-us-textiles.json: the current V1 Python client (backend/draps_client.py:_build_config_data) sends tariff_current=tariff_peak (flat). The escalation values 0.50 -> 0.60 below match the design intent recorded in design-v1-config-architecture.md §3.1 and reflect V1's underlying Postman JS calibration, not the Python wrapper. Reconcile when dispatch=v2_direct activates.
_notes.source_of_calibration: Calibration coefficients (constant -35.9048 / linear 133.6627 / quadratic -116.1008) are sector-level (textile quadratic), reused verbatim from india-us-textiles per design-v1-config-architecture.md §3.1.
_notes.iter4_role: First non-India commodity leaf. Proves: (a) the corridor + commodity merge resolves cleanly for VN-US-tex via the same _base → vietnam-us → vietnam-us-textiles chain that India uses; (b) the resolved profile satisfies the same JSON Schema + N3c cross-file checks that lock india-us-textiles; (c) the sovereign spread is the only corridor-level value that differs between the two leaves — visible in test_composer_derived.py's frozen fixture.

source.note: Identical to india-us-textiles.json (importer = US in both corridors). Redeclared here for explicitness in the commodity leaf, but a no-op against the corridor file's merge.

source.citation: Armington 3.8 from GTAP 11 (Corong et al. 2017), sector-level so identical to india-us-textiles. Tariff levels 0.50 / 0.60 held identical to india-us-textiles by Iter-4 design choice (see _notes.iter4_demo_choice). Calibration coefficients reused verbatim from india-us-textiles (V1 inline CALIBRATION) — sector-level quadratic, not country-specific.

S&P BB+ Local Currency LT (June 28 2025, stable). 100bps base / 140bps peak: 2x the V1 India-BBB anchor (50/70bps) reflecting the BB+ vs BBB ratings-ladder gap and the speculative-grade boundary crossing. Methodology placeholder pending Iter-9 live CDS binding.

V1 textile DSO 60d normal / 90d stressed. Reused verbatim from india-us-textiles since this is a sector-level proxy not country-specific in V1; Vietnam textile-DSO snapshots land in Iter-9 alongside live data binding.
